data.title: Why Is Vanguard Total Stock Market (VTI) ETF Rising Today, 5/29/26?
data.description:
The Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF (VTI), which tracks the broader U.S. stock market, rose 0.35% at Friday's market open after reports of a possible peace agreement involving Iran boosted investor sentiment. Meanwhile, oil prices moved lower following reports of a proposed 60-day ceasefire extension and the potential easing of shipping restrictions through the Strait of Hormuz, although tensions between the U.S. and Iran remain unresolved.

data.title: Why Is Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO) Up Today, 5/29/2026?
data.description:
The Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO), which tracks the S&P 500 Index (SPX), inched higher by 0.13% in pre-market trading on Friday as traders monitored developments in the Middle East.

data.title: Dow Jones Climbs with U.S.-Iran 'Very Close' to a Deal
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is set to open Friday higher as the U.S. and Iran inch forward toward a peace deal.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Vice President J.D. Vance said the two sides are "very close" to reaching an agreement that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz, extend the ceasefire by 60 days, and commence nuclear negotiations. "We're going back and forth on a couple of language points," he said. "We've made a lot of progress here."

Deal Progress Sends Oil and Gas Lower

Axios reported on Wednesday that negotiators from the two countries had agreed to the terms of a memorandum of understanding (MOU), although it had not yet received final approval from President Trump. Trump was briefed on the matter and requested a few days to consider it.

Oil and gas prices are sinking amid investor optimism, with Brent crude (BZ) futures down by over 2%. Gas prices across the U.S. dropped by 3.5 cents per gallon overnight, marking the largest single-day drop in 17 years.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Why Is Penny Stock PRF Technologies (PRFX) Up Today?
data.description:
PRF Technologies (PRFX) stock rocketed higher on Friday after the AI company provided an update on the development of DeepSolar Predict. This is the company's AI-driven renewable energy revenue optimization platform. The DeepSolar Predict platform is designed to help renewable energy operators move from production monitoring and standalone forecasting to real-time revenue optimization. It does so by combining AI forecasting, storage intelligence, and decision support.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

PRF Technologies revealed that DeepSolar Predict is advancing toward a commercial release. This would allow the company to generate revenue from the platform and help recoup the costs of its creation. Additionally, the company said that DeepSolar Predict builds on its other initiatives, including its participation in the Nvidia (NVDA) Connect program. Nvidia Connect is a free program that helps software development companies and service providers shorten time to market through tailored development resources.

Dr. Ehud Geller, chairman of PRF Technologies, said the renewable energy market is undergoing a change. That includes accounting for financial exposure and not just energy volume. As such, the company will focus on helping customers "move from reactive operations to proactive, data-driven revenue optimization."

PRF Technologies Stock Movement Today

PRF Technologies stock was up nearly 230% on Friday, following a 1.48% jump yesterday. However, the stock has fallen 57.29% year-to-date and also dropped 85.8% over the past 12 months.

PRFX stock trading activity today saw some 23 million shares change hands. That's well above the company's three-month average daily trading volume of about 186,000 shares.

Is PRF Technologies Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, traditional analyst coverage of PRFX stock is lacking. Fortunately, TipRanks' AI analyst Spark has it covered. Spark rates PRFX stock as Underperform with a $1.50 price target. It cites "weak financial fundamentals (minimal revenue, ongoing losses, and cash burn) and bearish technicals (price below key moving averages with negative MACD)" as reasons for this stance.
data.author: William White

data.title: OpenAI Arms Japan's Top Banks With GPT-5.5 to Thwart New Breed of AI Hacks
data.description:
OpenAI, the tech firm behind ChatGPT, has granted major Japanese banks access to its latest GPT-5.5 artificial intelligence model as concerns grow about AI-driven threats. The move comes after Japanese officials and OpenAI executives discussed ways to bolster the country's defenses against growing cyberattacks.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

NVDS: built for a short position on NVDA

Japanese Banks Gain Access to GPT-5.5 for Cybersecurity

Japan's Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama confirmed the update after meeting with OpenAI Chief Strategy Officer Jason Kwon in Tokyo. Katayama said the new access is a big step toward improving the cybersecurity skills of Japanese financial firms.

Notably, the release of new, more advanced AI models capable of high-level code has increased hackers' ability to identify bugs in systems and exploit them faster than before. To combat this defense threat, major banks and other firms are working to gain early access to these advanced AI models before hackers do. 

Currently, some of Japan's largest banks, including MUFG Bank (MUFG), Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corporation (SMFG), and Mizuho Bank (MFG), will soon gain access to OpenAI's GPT-5.5 model. The powerful tool is believed to be just as capable as Anthropic's Claude Mythos, its industry rival. 

OpenAI Expands AI Security Partnerships Globally

OpenAI's decision to open access to its AI models comes as governments and financial firms worldwide respond to the growing cybersecurity risks. Japanese officials claimed that using these advanced AI models could help them better defend themselves against threats.

At the same time, Japan has launched a new public-private cybersecurity working group to protect the country's financial system from AI-related threats. Officials said the initiative will also review risks linked to rival AI systems, including Anthropic's Mythos model.

Notably, OpenAI's latest move with Japanese banks comes after its talks to allow some European firms to use its model for cybersecurity and defense purposes. The private company is also limiting broader access to its GPT-5.5 model as it carefully selects those who can use it to avoid potential risks.

What Are the AI Stocks to Buy Now?

Wall Street analysts rate Nvidia (NVDA), Alphabet Class A (GOOGL), and Microsoft (MSFT) as strong buys, based on TipRanks consensus data. Among these stocks, NVDA has the highest upside potential of 43.86% and an average price target of $308.22 over the past 12 months. For more information on these stocks' performances, ratings, and price targets, visit the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Stock Futures Rise as Investors Monitor Middle East Conflict; Oil Prices Fall
data.description:
U.S. stock futures trended higher on Friday morning as traders monitored developments in the Middle East. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.04%, 0.24%, and 0.09%, respectively, at 8:35 a.m. EDT on May 29.

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

There were reports that the U.S. and Iran had agreed to a 60-day memorandum to extend the ceasefire, though the deal still awaits Trump's approval. However, Iranian state media reported missile launches, adding to concerns about a fragile ceasefire.

At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) slipped about 1.75% to $92.05 a barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) fell around 1.40% to $87.88.

In Thursday's regular trading session, all three major U.S. indices closed at record highs for a second straight day after April inflation came in softer-than-expected. The Nasdaq Composite, the S&P 500, and the Dow Jones rose 0.91%, 0.58%, and 0.05%, respectively.

Turning to trending stocks, Dell Technologies (DELL) rallied more than 34% after the PC and server maker raised its full-year guidance. American Eagle Outfitters (AEO) stock plunged 13% following disappointing first-quarter sales. The company's second-quarter guidance failed to impress investors.

Gap (GAP) stock rose more than 15% after the clothing retailer slashed its full-year sales outlook. The company delivered mixed first-quarter results. Okta (OKTA) shares rose 8% after the identity management company issued upbeat guidance and reported better-than-expected first-quarter results. Meanwhile, AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) stock plunged 13% on a Blue Origin rocket exploding on a launchpad during a ground test on Thursday night. ASTS has a partnership with Blue Origin. The news dragged down shares of several space companies, with Rocket Lab (RKLB) stock falling about 5% at the time of writing.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Could Trump Accounts Become Robinhood's Next Growth Driver? Here's Why Investors Are Paying Attention
data.description:
Robinhood (HOOD), the online brokerage and investing platform, stock moved higher after announcing that the official Trump Accounts app is now available for download. Investors appear to be looking beyond the app launch and focusing on the potential for Trump Accounts to bring billions of dollars in new assets to Robinhood over time.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

The launch marks an important step for the new government-backed savings program, which is set to begin on July 4. Robinhood is serving as trustee for the accounts and will hold assets on behalf of participating children. The company said the first activation emails have already been sent by the U.S. Treasury, with more invitations expected in the coming weeks.

The initiative has already attracted support from major business leaders. Dell Technologies (DELL) founder and CEO Michael Dell has pledged $6.25 billion to help fund Trump Accounts.

Why Investors Are Watching

Trump Accounts are tax-advantaged investment accounts designed for U.S. children under the age of 18.

Children born between 2025 and 2028 are eligible for a one-time $1,000 contribution from the U.S. government. Family members, friends, and employers can then contribute up to $5,000 per year.

All funds are automatically invested in low-cost index funds tied to the broader U.S. stock market, allowing savings to grow over time.

A New Growth Opportunity for Robinhood?

For investors, the bigger story may be what the program could mean for Robinhood's future.

As trustee, Robinhood could oversee a growing pool of assets if enrollment remains strong. The accounts are built for long-term investing, which could help increase the company's assets under custody over time.

The program may also introduce millions of young Americans and their families to Robinhood's platform, creating another avenue for future growth.

With the official launch date now just weeks away, investors will be watching closely to see how many families enroll and whether the program becomes a meaningful source of long-term asset growth for Robinhood.

Is HOOD Stock a Buy? 

The stock of Robinhood has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 18 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 15 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average HOOD price target of $100.48 implies 18% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Is Nvidia Stock Cheaper Than AMD Right Now? Here's What the Numbers Show
data.description:
Following another strong earnings report, Nvidia (NVDA) has once again left investors wondering how much higher the stock can climb. Even after the stock's massive rally over the past year, valuation metrics suggest Nvidia currently looks cheaper than rival Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) based on forward earnings estimates.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

High conviction NVDA bears now have this Tradr ETF

For analysts, one of the most closely watched measures is the forward price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio, which compares a company's current stock price to expected earnings over the next 12 months.

Nvidia Trades at a Lower Forward Valuation

Even with Nvidia's dominant position in AI chips and data centers, the stock currently trades at a lower forward P/E multiple than AMD. Notably, Nvidia currently trades at roughly 33x to 39x forward earnings, while AMD trades closer to about 58x to 63x forward earnings.

That means investors are currently paying a higher premium for AMD's projected earnings despite Nvidia generating much larger revenue, profit, and cash flow from the AI boom.

Nvidia vs. AMD: Latest Earnings Comparison

The difference becomes more visible when comparing the companies' latest quarterly results released in May 2026.

Nvidia's latest results showed just how far ahead the company remains in the AI chip race. Revenue jumped 85% year over year to $81.6 billion, driven mainly by explosive demand for its AI data center chips. The company's data center business alone generated $75.2 billion in revenue, while profits and cash flow also surged sharply.

AMD also delivered solid growth, especially in its data center segment, as demand for AI servers and inference chips continued improving. However, the company's overall scale remains far smaller than Nvidia's. AMD reported total revenue of $10.3 billion and data center revenue of $5.8 billion for the quarter.

Why Nvidia May Look Cheaper Than AMD Right Now

Even though Nvidia stock has surged during the AI boom, the company's profits and revenue have grown just as quickly. Because Nvidia's earnings are rising so quickly, the stock's valuation has stayed lower than many investors might expect despite the massive rally.

Meanwhile, AMD continues to attract investors because of its growing presence in AI servers and inference chips. However, despite generating much smaller revenue and profit than Nvidia, AMD currently trades at a noticeably higher forward earnings multiple. That is why some investors believe Nvidia may actually offer better value at current levels.

Which Is the Better AI Chip Stock, According to Analysts?

Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, Nvidia appears to have the stronger upside potential right now, with Wall Street analysts expecting about 43% upside. In comparison, analysts currently see roughly 7% downside for Advanced Micro Devices.

Investors can use the comparison below to better evaluate which AI chip stock fits their investment strategy.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
